name: inception_next

members.name: InceptionNeXtConfig
members.signature: InceptionNeXtConfig(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, depths: tuple[int, ...] = (3, 3, 9, 3), dims: tuple[int, ...] = (96, 192, 384, 768), band_kernel: int = 11, mlp_ratios: tuple[int, ...] = (4, 4, 4, 3))

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, depths: tuple[int, ...] = (3, 3, 9, 3), dims: tuple[int, ...] = (96, 192, 384, 768), band_kernel: int = 11, mlp_ratios: tuple[int, ...] = (4, 4, 4, 3))

members.summary: Configuration dataclass for every InceptionNeXt variant.
members.extended:
`InceptionNeXtConfig` is an immutable container that fully
specifies the architecture of an InceptionNeXt (Yu et al., 2024).
InceptionNeXt is a drop-in replacement for the ConvNeXt block that
factorizes the single large $7 \times 7$ depthwise
convolution into four parallel Inception-style branches:

$$
\mathrm{IDWConv}(x) = \mathrm{Concat}\bigl(
    x^{(1)},\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{3 \times 3}(x^{(2)}),\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{1 \times K}(x^{(3)}),\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{K \times 1}(x^{(4)})\bigr).
$$

The remaining elements of the ConvNeXt block (BatchNorm, inverted-
bottleneck Conv-MLP, layer scale, residual) are kept unchanged, so
the Tiny / Small / Base variants reuse the same depth and width
tables as ConvNeXt while running noticeably faster.

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description:
Number of output classes for the classification head.  Defaults
to `1000` (ImageNet-1k).

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of input image channels.  Defaults to `3` (RGB).

parameters.name: depths
parameters.description:
Number of MetaNeXt blocks in each of the four stages.  Defaults
to `(3, 3, 9, 3)` (InceptionNeXt-T).

parameters.name: dims
parameters.description:
Channel width per stage.  Defaults to `(96, 192, 384, 768)`
(InceptionNeXt-T).

parameters.name: band_kernel
parameters.description:
Kernel length $K$ of the horizontal $1 \times K$
and vertical $K \times 1$ *band* depthwise convolutions
inside the Inception token mixer.  Defaults to `11`.

parameters.name: mlp_ratios
parameters.description:
Per-stage MLP expansion ratios.  Defaults to `(4, 4, 4, 3)`
— the reference recipe uses ratio 3 in the final stage to
match the head's compute budget.

examples:
Build an InceptionNeXt-T configuration with a 100-class head:
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import InceptionNeXtConfig
>>> cfg = InceptionNeXtConfig(num_classes=100)
>>> cfg.depths, cfg.dims, cfg.band_kernel, cfg.num_classes
((3, 3, 9, 3), (96, 192, 384, 768), 11, 100)
notes:
Reference: Weihao Yu *et al.*, *"InceptionNeXt: When Inception
Meets ConvNeXt"*, CVPR 2024,
[arXiv:2303.16900](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.16900).

attributes.name: model_type
attributes.description: Constant string `"inception_next"` used by the model registry.

members.name: InceptionNeXt
members.extended:
A drop-in replacement for the ConvNeXt backbone that factorizes
the single large $7 \times 7$ depthwise conv into four
parallel Inception-style branches operating on disjoint channel
splits:

$$
\mathrm{IDWConv}(x) = \mathrm{Concat}\bigl(
    x^{(1)},\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{3 \times 3}(x^{(2)}),\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{1 \times K}(x^{(3)}),\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{K \times 1}(x^{(4)})\bigr),
$$

with $K = \texttt{band\_kernel}$ (default 11).  The four
branches together preserve a large effective receptive field
while cutting depthwise FLOPs and memory traffic relative to a
single $7 \times 7$ depthwise conv.  Each MetaNeXtBlock
further applies BatchNorm, a 1x1 Conv-MLP, and a layer-scale
parameter on the residual branch.

`forward_features` returns the global-average-pooled
$(B, \texttt{dims[-1]})$ feature.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen dataclass specifying `depths`, `dims`,
`band_kernel`, `mlp_ratios`, `in_channels`, and
`num_classes`.  See `InceptionNeXtConfig`.

examples:
Build an InceptionNeXt-T backbone and run a forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import (
...     InceptionNeXt, InceptionNeXtConfig,
... )
>>> model = InceptionNeXt(InceptionNeXtConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape                       # (B, dims[-1])
(1, 768)
notes:
Reference: Weihao Yu *et al.*, *"InceptionNeXt: When Inception
Meets ConvNeXt"*, CVPR 2024,
[arXiv:2303.16900](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.16900).

attributes.name: stages
attributes.description:
Four `_Stage` modules, each containing an optional
downsampler and a sequence of MetaNeXt blocks.

members.name: InceptionNeXtForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)

members.summary: InceptionNeXt with an MLP classification head (Yu et al., 2024).
members.extended:
Wraps the same trunk as `InceptionNeXt` (patchify stem +
four stages of MetaNeXt blocks) and adds the reference-recipe MLP
classifier head: global average pool → `Linear(dim, 3*dim)` →
GELU → LayerNorm → `Linear(3*dim, num_classes)`:

$$
\text{logits} = W_2\,
    \mathrm{LN}\!\bigl(\mathrm{GELU}(W_1\,
    \mathrm{GAP}(z^{L}))\bigr) + b_2.
$$

Pass `labels` to `forward` to compute the cross-entropy
loss in the same pass.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture specification.  Must set `num_classes` to the
desired number of output categories.  See
`InceptionNeXtConfig`.

examples:
End-to-end inference with the default InceptionNeXt-T classifier:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import (
...     InceptionNeXtConfig, InceptionNeXtForImageClassification,
... )
>>> model = InceptionNeXtForImageClassification(InceptionNeXtConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Reference: Weihao Yu *et al.*, *"InceptionNeXt: When Inception
Meets ConvNeXt"*, CVPR 2024.  InceptionNeXt-T matches or exceeds
ConvNeXt-T accuracy at noticeably lower wall-clock latency.

members.name: inception_next_base
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *InceptionNeXt-B* configuration:
`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`, `dims=(128, 256, 512, 1024)`,
`band_kernel=11`.  Approximately **87M parameters** — the widest
of the paper's three main variants.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available in the model zoo.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides applied on top of the canonical
InceptionNeXt-B config.

returns.description:
An `InceptionNeXt` backbone returning a flat
$(B, 1024)$ feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import inception_next_base
>>> model = inception_next_base()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape
(1, 1024)
notes:
InceptionNeXt-B reaches **84.0% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Yu et al.,
2024, Table 2).  See
[arXiv:2303.16900](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.16900).

members.name: inception_next_base_cls
members.signature: inception_next_base_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: InceptionNeXtBaseWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `inception_next_base` backbone with the
reference MLP classifier head (`Linear → GELU → LayerNorm →
Linear`).  Default output is `num_classes=1000` (ImageNet-1k).
Approximately **87M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`InceptionNeXtBaseWeights.SAIL_IN1K`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive
with `weights` (which wins if both given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical InceptionNeXt-B config.

returns.description:
Classifier returning `ImageClassificationOutput` whose
`logits` has shape `(B, num_classes)`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import inception_next_base_cls
>>> model = inception_next_base_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
InceptionNeXt-B reaches **84.0% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Yu et al.,
2024, Table 2).  Pretrained weights are converted from timm's
`inception_next_base.sail_in1k` and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub
under `lucid-dl/inception-next-base`.

members.name: inception_next_small
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *InceptionNeXt-S* configuration:
`depths=(3, 3, 27, 3)`, `dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`,
`band_kernel=11`.  Approximately **49M parameters** —
InceptionNeXt-T widened in the third stage (9 → 27 blocks).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available in the model zoo.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides applied on top of the canonical
InceptionNeXt-S config.

returns.description:
An `InceptionNeXt` backbone returning a flat
$(B, 768)$ feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import inception_next_small
>>> model = inception_next_small()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape
(1, 768)
notes:
InceptionNeXt-S reaches **83.5% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Yu et al.,
2024, Table 2).  See
[arXiv:2303.16900](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.16900).

members.name: inception_next_small_cls
members.signature: inception_next_small_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: InceptionNeXtSmallWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `inception_next_small` backbone with the
reference MLP classifier head (`Linear → GELU → LayerNorm →
Linear`).  Default output is `num_classes=1000` (ImageNet-1k).
Approximately **49M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`InceptionNeXtSmallWeights.SAIL_IN1K`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive
with `weights` (which wins if both given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical InceptionNeXt-S config.

returns.description:
Classifier returning `ImageClassificationOutput` whose
`logits` has shape `(B, num_classes)`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import inception_next_small_cls
>>> model = inception_next_small_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
InceptionNeXt-S reaches **83.5% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Yu et al.,
2024, Table 2).  Pretrained weights are converted from timm's
`inception_next_small.sail_in1k` and hosted on the Hugging Face
Hub under `lucid-dl/inception-next-small`.

members.name: inception_next_tiny
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *InceptionNeXt-T* configuration:
`depths=(3, 3, 9, 3)`, `dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`,
`band_kernel=11`.  Approximately **28M parameters** —
ConvNeXt-T compatible, but with each $7 \times 7$ depthwise
conv replaced by the four-branch InceptionDWConv mixer.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available in the model zoo.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides applied on top of the canonical
InceptionNeXt-T config.  Each override must match a field of
`InceptionNeXtConfig`.

returns.description:
An `InceptionNeXt` backbone returning a flat
$(B, 768)$ feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import inception_next_tiny
>>> model = inception_next_tiny()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape
(1, 768)
notes:
InceptionNeXt-T matches ConvNeXt-T accuracy
(**82.3% top-1 on ImageNet-1k**, Yu et al., 2024, Table 2) at
noticeably lower latency.  See
[arXiv:2303.16900](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.16900).

members.name: inception_next_tiny_cls
members.signature: inception_next_tiny_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: InceptionNeXtTinyWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `inception_next_tiny` backbone with the
reference MLP classifier head (`Linear → GELU → LayerNorm →
Linear`).  Default output is `num_classes=1000` (ImageNet-1k).
Approximately **28M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`InceptionNeXtTinyWeights.SAIL_IN1K`);
a tag string (e.g. `"SAIL_IN1K"`) → that specific checkpoint.
Mutually exclusive with `weights` (which wins if both given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical InceptionNeXt-T config.

returns.description:
Classifier returning `ImageClassificationOutput` whose
`logits` has shape `(B, num_classes)`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.inception_next import inception_next_tiny_cls
>>> model = inception_next_tiny_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
InceptionNeXt-T reaches **82.3% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Yu et al.,
2024, Table 2).  Pretrained weights are converted from timm's
`inception_next_tiny.sail_in1k` and hosted on the Hugging Face Hub
under `lucid-dl/inception-next-tiny`.

members.name: InceptionNeXtBaseWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`SAIL_IN1K`) converted
from timm's `inception_next_base.sail_in1k` weights and re-hosted
under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/inception-next-base`.  Uses a
tighter `crop_pct=0.95` eval pipeline (236 resize / 224 crop).

members.name: InceptionNeXtSmallWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`SAIL_IN1K`) converted
from timm's `inception_next_small.sail_in1k` weights and re-hosted
under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/inception-next-small`.

members.name: InceptionNeXtTinyWeights
members.extended:
Ships a single ImageNet-1k checkpoint (`SAIL_IN1K`) converted
from timm's `inception_next_tiny.sail_in1k` weights and re-hosted
under `huggingface.co/lucid-dl/inception-next-tiny`.

citation: Yu, Weihao, et al. "InceptionNeXt: When Inception Meets ConvNeXt." Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, 2024.
theory:
InceptionNeXt is a drop-in replacement for the ConvNeXt block that
eliminates the cost of a single large $7 \times 7$ depthwise
convolution by *factorizing* it into four parallel, low-cost
Inception-style branches.  The motivation is that large-kernel
depthwise convs dominate ConvNeXt's wall-clock latency on modern
accelerators despite their relatively small FLOP count: depthwise
convolutions are memory-bandwidth bound and large kernels amplify
that bottleneck.

The *InceptionDWConv2d* operator splits the input channels into
four groups and applies in parallel: (i) an identity branch,
(ii) a small $3 \times 3$ square depthwise conv, (iii) a
horizontal *band* depthwise conv of shape $1 \times K$, and
(iv) a vertical band depthwise conv of shape $K \times 1$,
with $K = 11$ by default.  The outputs are concatenated along
the channel dimension:



$$
\mathrm{IDWConv}(x) = \mathrm{Concat}\bigl(
    x^{(1)},\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{3 \times 3}(x^{(2)}),\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{1 \times K}(x^{(3)}),\;
    \mathrm{DW}_{K \times 1}(x^{(4)})\bigr).
$$

The factorization preserves a large effective receptive field
(the band convolutions span $K$ pixels along each axis) while
cutting depthwise FLOPs and — more importantly — memory traffic.
All other elements of the ConvNeXt block (LayerNorm, inverted
bottleneck MLP, layer scale, residual) are kept unchanged, so the
Tiny / Small / Base variants reuse the same depth and width tables
as ConvNeXt while running noticeably faster at equal accuracy.
